name: Randy Weston
abstract: Randolph Edward "Randy" Weston (April 6, 1926 – September 1, 2018) was an American jazz pianist and composer whose creativity was inspired by his ancestral African connection. Weston's piano style owed much to Duke Ellington and Thelonious Monk, whom he cited in a 2018 video as among pianists he counted as influences, as well as Count Ba…

interviews.title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Randy Weston was one of my mentors also. He taught me a lot about the business — publishing, and how publishing works.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been playing with Randy Weston for about the last 15 years
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: We all – when I say we, I’m talking about some of the CTI artists – went to Tangier... Randy Weston, who fortunately is still with us... The next project is March and April of 1972, CTI, Blue Moses. I played on that too, right?
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Melba Liston
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been writing for Randy. I’m still writing for Randy. I wrote for him up until I had the stroke.
about_quotes.doc_title: Melba Liston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] You worked with Randy Weston. [INTERVIEWEE] For almost a year.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: I had, no, I look around and saw that Miles was looking at these things and I was…and he said, “Oh, Randy! I’m glad somebody got around to recording him. It should have been done along time ago,” and I said, “Well if you feel that way about Randy, why didn’t you recommend him to your label?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
